prompt:
Read the workflow below and follow it. Task ID: GROVE-S03-T06.

---

## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Add a `verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` function plus `LockVerifyStatus` enum and `LockVerifyEntry` struct to `core/src/registry.rs`, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New `pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing }` in registry.rs; New `pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status }` in registry.rs; New `pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` — Ok(None) when lock absent, Ok(Some(vec)) when parsed; Wasm resolved via `registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm` (same path as `resolve()`; covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY); NotFound error on wasm path → Missing; hash mismatch → Mismatch; equal hashes → Match; Reuses existing `sha256()` primitive so a lock written by `write_lock_for` verifies clean; Four unit tests: absent-lock (Ok(None)), Match after write_lock_for, Mismatch on tampered hash, Missing on nonexistent lang wasm; Add `pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus}` to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; No new files, no Cargo.toml changes, no lock-format changes — purely additive; Not material: additive read-only primitive, no CLI surface, no version bump required
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Assess the plan to add a read-only grove.lock wasm sha256 verify primitive (verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) to core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Verified against actual code: lock JSON shape {version,grammars:[{name,version,wasm}]} matches write_lock_for@412; plan's parse of doc[grammars]/entry[wasm]/name is correct.; AC#2 holds: wasm_sha256@106 == sha256(self.wasm) and verify_lock recomputes sha256 over the same file bytes, so a fresh lock verifies all-Match.; Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm equals resolve@219's idx.root.join(lang)/grammar.wasm in steady state (index root seeded from registry_root).; Missing/Mismatch/None signals map correctly to NotFound / hash-diff / absent-file; Ok(None) is a clean non-error signal (AC#3).; Change is purely additive (new public types+fn, no signature/lock-format change) — matches 'no version bump' operational impact.; Advisory: index() memoizes root via OnceLock while verify_lock re-evaluates registry_root(); keep the Match test registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process GROVE_REGISTRY mutation) to avoid divergence.; Advisory: decide malformed-entry handling (entry missing wasm/name) deliberately; avoid silent unwrap.; Advisory: verify_lock takes the lock file path (like locked_langs), not a project root — T07 must pass the resolved grove.lock path.; All five acceptance criteria addressed; no changes required before implementation.
Findings: See key_changes — all findings mirrored there for schema compliance.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Add verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> plus LockVerifyStatus enum and LockVerifyEntry struct to core/src/registry.rs, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: Added pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing } to registry.rs; Added pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status } to registry.rs; Added pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> — Ok(None) when lock absent; Wasm resolved via registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm (same path as resolve()); NotFound I/O error → Missing; hash equality → Match; hash mismatch → Mismatch; Other I/O errors propagated via anyhow with context; Added verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent unit test; Added verify_lock_matches_after_write_lock_for unit test (fresh lock verifies all Match); Added verify_lock_detects_tampered_hash unit test (mutated wasm field → Mismatch); Added verify_lock_detects_missing_wasm unit test (nonexistent lang → Missing); Added pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; All 165 unit tests pass; 4 new verify_lock tests all green
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Reviewed the additive verify_lock primitive plus LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry types in core/src/registry.rs and the lib.rs re-export against the approved plan.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: All 8 plan/AC items met: types, verify_lock signature, wasm path resolution, status mapping, hash-format consistency, curated re-export, purely additive.; Independently ran cargo test verify_lock: 4 passed, 0 failed (161 filtered = 165 total, matches claim).; cargo clippy -p grove-cst --release --locked: clean, zero warnings.; Ok(None) on absent lock; NotFound->Missing; hash-eq->Match; diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagated with anyhow context (no silent swallow).; Fresh lock verifies all-Match: verify_lock reuses sha256() and write_lock_for pins wasm_sha256() delegating to same primitive.; Malformed entries (missing name/wasm) are continue-skipped, not unwrapped — deliberate non-panicking choice per plan-review advisory.; Doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.; Tests authentic and meaningful (round-trip Match, tampered->Mismatch, nonexistent->Missing, absent->None).; Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated; fine for read-only advisory primitive.; Advisory (non-blocking): temp-test files share process id but use distinct name stems; no collision.
Findings: All 8 plan/AC items met and independently verified.; cargo test verify_lock: 4/4 pass; clippy clean.; Correct Match/Mismatch/Missing/None signal mapping; errors propagated not swallowed.; Additive-only: no signature, lock-format, or Cargo.toml changes.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validated that verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry are correctly implemented in core/src/registry.rs against all five acceptance criteria.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: AC#1 PASS: verify_lock returns Ok(None) for absent lock file; test verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent passes.; AC#2 PASS: All three status variants (Match/Mismatch/Missing) tested and passing; assertions are specific.; AC#3 PASS: verify_lock calls sha256(&bytes); write_lock_for uses wasm_sha256() delegating to same sha256 — round-trip Match test confirms.; AC#4 PASS: Exactly 4 unit tests covering absent-lock, Match, Mismatch, Missing — all 4 pass.; AC#5 PASS: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test 287 total 0 failed; files end with newline.; Public surface: pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} confirmed at lib.rs:70.; Error handling: NotFound->Missing, other I/O errors propagated via anyhow, malformed entries skipped.; Additive only: no existing signatures, lock format, or Cargo.toml changed. No regressions.
Full artifact: VALIDATION_REPORT.md

# Approve Task
## Iron Laws

- Approve only when the implementation is consistent with the project's architecture and the deployment posture is understood. Architectural sign-off is not a rubber stamp — it is the last point at which cross-cutting concerns can be caught cheaply.
- Read `.forge/personas/architect.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0a. Pre-flight Gate Check:
   - **Entity-mode resolution:** read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`. All store-cli calls below substitute `{entity_kind}` and `{record_id}` for the literal "task"/{taskId} placeholders.
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/preflight-gate.cjs --phase approve --{entity_kind} {record_id}`
   - Exit 1 (gate failed) → print stderr and HALT. Do not proceed; do not attempt to produce the artifact.
   - Exit 2 (misconfiguration) → print stderr and HALT.
   - Exit 0 → continue.

0b. Pipeline Step Guard (user-invoked state check):
   - If `--force` is present in the invocation arguments, skip this step entirely.
   - If `entity_kind == "bug"`, skip this step entirely (bug state is managed by meta-fix-bug.md).
   - Read current task state:
     `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs read task {record_id} --fields status`
   - Extract the `status` field from the JSON output.
   - Allowed states for this phase: `review-approved`.
   - If the current status is NOT in the allowed set:
     Print the following and HALT (do not proceed):
     `× Task {record_id} is in state '{status}' — /forge:review-code (or /forge:validate) must complete first. To run the full pipeline: /forge:run-task {record_id}`

1. Load Context:
   - Read task prompt
   - Read final PLAN.md
   - Read approved CODE_REVIEW.md
   - Read PROGRESS.md

2. Architectural Review:
   - Verify implementation aligns with project architecture
   - Check for cross-cutting concerns (impact on other modules)
   - Assess operational impact (deployment changes, migrations)

3. Sign Off:
   - Write the architect approval via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"architect-approval", content:"<markdown>" })`
     The markdown content must contain:
     - A canonical verdict line for human readers, on its own line, in this exact form:
       ```
       **Verdict:** [Approved | Revision Required]
       ```
     - Approval status rationale
     - Deployment notes
     - Follow-up items for future sprints
   - The downstream commit-phase preflight gate does NOT read this markdown. **Task mode:** it reads `task.status === "approved"` set in step 4. **Bug mode:** it reads `bug.summaries.approve.verdict === "approved"` set in step 5. The `**Verdict:**` line is a human breadcrumb only.

4. Finalize:
   - Transitions:
     - **Task mode** — Update status: `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status approved`. The status IS the verdict signal for task-mode commit gate (`STATUS_SOURCE` in `read-verdict.cjs`).
     - **Bug mode** — NO status write. The bug remains `in-progress`. The verdict signal travels through `summaries.approve.verdict` written in step 5 below (read by `read-verdict.cjs § BUG_PHASE_VERDICT_SOURCE`). Writing `bug.status` here — especially writing `approved` or `verified` — violates `meta-fix-bug.md § Iron Laws #2` and is the trap that produced the FORGE-BUG-002 regression.
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.

5. Emit Summary Sidecar:
   - Write the approve summary via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"approve-summary", content:"<JSON>" })`
     The JSON content must have the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what this approval covered>",
       "findings":    ["<up to 12 bullets, 200 chars each — architectural notes, deployment concerns>"],
       "verdict":     "<approved | revision>",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>",
       "artifact_ref":"ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md"
     }
     ```
   - Call (task mode) — optional for tasks, since `task.status` is the canonical signal.
     The sidecar path is auto-resolved from the record's `path` — never pass it:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-summary {taskId} approve
     ```
     Or (bug mode) — REQUIRED for bugs, this is the canonical verdict signal:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-bug-summary {bugId} approve
     ```
   - In bug mode, if the set-bug-summary call exits non-zero, fix the sidecar JSON and retry. Do not return without a valid summary — the downstream commit gate has no other way to read the approval verdict.
```

<!-- See _fragments/generation-instructions.md for Generation Instructions template -->

content.text:
🗻 **grove Architect** — I hold the shape of the whole. I give final sign-off before commit.

## Identity

I am the grove Architect. I plan sprints, approve completed tasks, and maintain architectural coherence across the project. I have final sign-off before code is committed.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):
```bash
node .forge/tools/banners.cjs north
```
## What I Need to Know

- The full architecture of the project
- The business domain and entity model
- The current sprint's goals and priorities
- Historical complexity patterns from previous sprints
- Cross-cutting concerns and technical debt

## What I Produce

- Sprint manifests — task breakdown with dependencies, estimates, priorities
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` — final sign-off on completed tasks
- Architecture decisions and updates to knowledge base

## Capabilities

- Plan sprints with dependency graphs
- Approve or reject completed tasks
- Update architecture documentation
- Identify cross-task conflicts and dependencies
## Project Context

- **Entity model and service boundaries**: See `engineering/architecture/` and `engineering/architecture/../business-domain/entity-model.md`
- **ID format and prefix convention**: Task IDs use the GROVE prefix from `.forge/config.json`
- **Technical debt areas**: 
- **Deployment topology**: See `engineering/architecture/deployment.md`
- **Operational impact categories**: 

## Commands

- **Syntax check**: `cargo test --release --locked`
- **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`

## Installed Skill Wiring

🗻 **grove Architect** — I hold the shape of the whole. I give final sign-off before commit.

Now the pre-flight gate check and status guard.
Gate passed, status is `review-approved` — allowed. Now load context.

- **registry_root re-eval vs index() OnceLock memoization**: the Match test is registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process env mutation), so the advised divergence risk does not materialize.
- **path is lock-file, not project root**: doc comment on `verify_lock` explicitly states this and directs T07 to pass the resolved `grove.lock` path. ✅

arguments.content:
# Architect Approval — GROVE-S03-T06

**Verdict:** Approved

## Rationale

The task adds a read-only `verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>`
primitive plus `LockVerifyStatus` and `LockVerifyEntry` to `core/src/registry.rs`,
re-exported through the curated `core/src/lib.rs` surface. It recomputes each cached
grammar wasm's sha256 and compares against the pinned `grove.lock`, enabling grove
doctor (T07) to detect drift.

The change is architecturally coherent:

- **Additive only.** No existing signatures, lock-file format, or `Cargo.toml`
  dependencies change. Nothing downstream is forced to migrate.
- **Reuses existing primitives.** Verification runs through the same `sha256()`
  helper that `write_lock_for`/`wasm_sha256()` pin with, so a freshly written lock
  round-trips to all-`Match` — no format skew risk between writer and verifier.
- **Path resolution is consistent** with `resolve()`: `registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm`,
  covering dev-stubs, cache_root, and `GROVE_REGISTRY`.
- **Signal mapping is clean and non-swallowing.** `Ok(None)` for absent lock,
  `NotFound → Missing`, hash-eq → `Match`, diff → `Mismatch`; other I/O errors
  propagate via anyhow context. Malformed entries are deliberately `continue`-skipped
  rather than unwrapped — a non-panicking choice the plan-review asked to be decided,
  and it is decided.

Independently corroborated by the code review (8/8 plan/AC items, clippy clean,
`verify_lock` 4/4) and validation (all five AC PASS, full suite green).

## Cross-Cutting Concerns

None. The primitive is isolated to the registry module and introduces no new
coupling. The doc comment correctly directs the T07 consumer to pass the resolved
`grove.lock` file path (not a project root), preventing a foreseeable misuse at the
integration boundary.

Advisory (non-blocking, carried forward): the lock top-level `version` field is not
validated. Acceptable for a read-only advisory primitive; if T07 begins gating on
lock schema version, fold that check in there rather than here.

## Deployment Notes

- **Version bump:** not required on its own (additive read-only primitive; ships
  under the sprint's aggregate release).
- **Regeneration:** none.
- **Migration:** none — lock format unchanged.
- **Security scan:** not required.

## Follow-Up Items

- **T07 (grove doctor):** consume `verify_lock` at the resolved `grove.lock` path;
  surface `Mismatch`/`Missing` as actionable diagnostics. This is the intended caller.
- Consider lock `version` validation if/when doctor gates on schema evolution.

content.text: Now finalize — task mode status transition, then write the approve summary.

content.text:
Task **GROVE-S03-T06** approved and finalized. 🗻

## Architect sign-off summary

**Verdict: Approved**

The task adds a purely additive, read-only `verify_lock(path)` primitive plus `LockVerifyStatus`/`LockVerifyEntry` to `core/src/registry.rs`, re-exported through the curated `lib.rs` surface. It recomputes each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compares against `grove.lock`, enabling grove doctor (T07) to detect drift.

**Why it clears architectural review:**
- **Additive only** — no signature, lock-format, or `Cargo.toml` changes; nothing downstream must migrate.
- **No writer/verifier skew** — reuses the same `sha256()` primitive as `write_lock_for`, so a fresh lock round-trips to all-`Match`.
- **Consistent path resolution** with `resolve()` (`registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm`), covering dev-stubs, cache_root, `GROVE_REGISTRY`.
- **Clean, non-swallowing signal mapping** — `Ok(None)`/`Missing`/`Match`/`Mismatch`, other I/O errors propagated via anyhow; malformed entries deliberately skipped.
- **No cross-cutting coupling**; doc comment steers T07 to the resolved lock path.
- Independently corroborated by code review (8/8, clippy clean) and validation (5/5 AC, suite green).

**Deployment:** no version bump on its own, no regeneration/migration/security scan.

**Follow-up:** T07 (grove doctor) is the intended consumer — surface `Mismatch`/`Missing` as actionable diagnostics; consider lock `version` validation there if doctor ever gates on schema evolution.

Finalization complete:
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` written (Verdict: Approved)
- `APPROVE-SUMMARY.json` written and merged via `set-summary`
- Task status transitioned `review-approved → approved` (the commit-gate signal)

Ready for the commit phase.
